card_page.policy_text: Weston Public Library: welcome to the least exclusive club in Weston. Are you looking for a private club with an exclusive, carefully curated member list? How about an institution where only the cream of the crop gets past the bouncers? Better yet, an invitation-only association where you need to pay a small fortune and your firstborn child in membership dues? Yeah, so we're NOT that. Exclusive membership? Nah. If you have a Massachusetts address, you make the cut. If you don't have a library card, just visit our Circulation Desk. Library card signup takes 5 minutes or less and all you need is something verifying your name and address, like a driver's license or school ID. If you don't have something like that, don't stress, just give us a call and we can figure out what you do have that will work. If you can't wait another minute to check out our digital resources, you can get a digital library card online and get instant access to our Overdrive/Libby eBook, magazine, and digital audiobook collection. This eCard is only good for 6 months, so plan to drop by the library to convert your eCard to a fancy-schmancy physical card that never expires. A membership that NEVER expires Yes, you read that right. Over the next few months, Minuteman Library Network will be doing away with library card expirations. As long as you use your card every now and again, it will remain active forever. We will still remind you to update us if your contact info changes. Speaking of that, have you moved? Got a new phone? Forgot the password to your old email and just abandoned the whole thing (not that we've ever done that)? Just call the circulation desk at 781-786-6150 and press 1 to have our staff update your account. You don't need to "know someone" to get into these events Genealogy Series Starts March 3 Register now! Finding Your Dream College With the Princeton Review Click to register. Zoom with local author, Joy Viola Click to register now Our library is a welcoming place. Open to all. Try to think of all the places that you can go to work, play, or just be. How many of those places don't expect you to buy anything or pay to get in? We bet you can't think of too many. Weston Public Library is a community gathering space that offers equal access to all comers. We hope that you will pop by and enjoy your Weston Public Library by settling into a comfy chair with a newspaper, sitting down to do a puzzle, borrowing a good book, or even checking out the Library of Things metal detector . Share on Facebook Share on Twitter Share via Email Copyright 2021 Weston, Mass. All Rights Reserved. 11 Town House Road, Weston, Mass. 02493 Comments can be sent to webmaster@westonma.gov Powered by

pass_page.policy_text:
To limit museum types being displayed, check the corresponding categories:
To select a museum, click “Details/Reserve”, then select which Pass to reserve.
{"@context": "http://schema.org","@type": "CivicStructure","address": "308 Congress Street, Boston, MA 02210","logo": "https://s3.amazonaws.com/ai-assets/museum-logos/ma-boston-childrens-museum_3_md.jpg","telephone": "617-426-6500","name": "Boston Children&apos;s Museum","url": "","description": "Founded in 1913 by the Science Teachers&apos; Bureau, the Boston Children&apos;s Museum is the second oldest children&apos;s museum in the world and one of the few that maintain a collection. Designed for both children and families, the Museum exhibits focus on science, culture, environmental awareness, health & fitness, and the arts, emphasizing hands-on engagement and learning through experience, and employing play as a tool to spark the inherent creativity, curiosity, and imagination of children."} Boston Children's Museum 308 Congress Street, Boston, MA 02210617-426-6500http://www.bostonchildrensmuseum.org/[Details/Reserve]
Boston Children's Museum Founded in 1913 by the Science Teachers' Bureau, the Boston Children's Museum is the second oldest children's museum in the world and one of the few that maintain a collection. Designed for both children and families, the Museum exhibits focus on science, culture, environmental awareness, health & fitness, and the arts, emphasizing hands-on engagement and learning through experience, and employing play as a tool to spark the inherent creativity, curiosity, and imagination of children.
Categories: Children, Crafts, Family, Technology
Pass Type: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
Pass Benefits Admits up to 4 people at half price per person.
Reserve a Pass Reserve a Pass
{"@context": "http://schema.org","@type": "CivicStructure","address": "15 State Street, Suite 1100, Boston, MA 02109","logo": "https://s3.amazonaws.com/ai-assets/museum-logos/ma-boston-harbor-islands_6_md.jpg","telephone": "617-223-8666","name": "Boston Harbor Island Ferry","url": "","description": "The largest recreational open space in Eastern Massachusetts, the Boston Harbor Islands National and State Park is comprised of 34 islands and mainland parks that deliver access to historic sites and provide opportunities to hike and camp. Transportation to the islands is by ferry and, depending on the location, your own boat. Please refer to their website for the most up-to-date information about what is open and how to get there."} Boston Harbor Island Ferry 15 State Street, Suite 1100, Boston, MA 02109617-223-8666http://www.bostonharborislands.org/[Details/Reserve]
Boston Harbor Island Ferry The largest recreational open space in Eastern Massachusetts, the Boston Harbor Islands National and State Park is comprised of 34 islands and mainland parks that deliver access to historic sites and provide opportunities to hike and camp. Transportation to the islands is by ferry and, depending on the location, your own boat. Please refer to their website for the most up-to-date information about what is open and how to get there.
Categories: Family, Nature, Recreation
Pass Types Available: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning), Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
Pass Benefits Apply the promo code for up to four "50%-off" tickets for Spectacle, Peddocks or Georges Island.
{"@context": "http://schema.org","@type": "CivicStructure","address": "210 Massachusetts Avenue, Boston, MA 02115","logo": "https://s3.amazonaws.com/ai-assets/museum-logos/ma-christian-science-plaza_65_md.png","telephone": "617-450-7224","name": "Christian Science Plaza","url": "","description": "The Christian Science Plaza is home to The Mother Church, The Christian Science Publishing Society, The Mary Baker Eddy Library, and the interactive <em>How Do You See the World?&trade;</em> experience with the <em>Mapparium&reg;</em> globe. The <em>How Do You See the World?&trade;</em> experience explores stories about global progress&mdash;how individuals worldwide have overcome challenges and found hope. Walk through the iconic <em>Mapparium&reg;</em> globe, and explore surrounding exhibits that feature stories from <em>The Christian Science Monitor</em> and the history of its founder, Mary Baker Eddy, who also discovered Christian Science. "} Christian Science Plaza 210 Massachusetts Avenue, Boston, MA 02115617-450-7224https://www.christianscience.com/find-us/visit-the-mother-church/visit-the-christian-science-plaza[Details/Reserve]
Christian Science Plaza The Christian Science Plaza is home to The Mother Church, The Christian Science Publishing Society, The Mary Baker Eddy Library, and the interactive How Do You See the World?™ experience with the Mapparium® globe. The How Do You See the World?™ experience explores stories about global progress—how individuals worldwide have overcome challenges and found hope. Walk through the iconic Mapparium® globe, and explore surrounding exhibits that feature stories from The Christian Science Monitor and the history of its founder, Mary Baker Eddy, who also discovered Christian Science.
Categories: Children, Family, History
Pass Type: Circulating Pass (must be picked up and returned to the branch)
Pass Benefits Each pass admits up to four people for general admission (Adult, Youth 6-17, Students, and Seniors). 
Admission for children 5 and younger is free. 
Not valid for special events or for public programming.
{"@context": "http://schema.org","@type": "CivicStructure","address": "200 Lexington Road, Concord, MA 01742","logo": "https://s3.amazonaws.com/ai-assets/museum-logos/ma-concord-museum_10_md.png","telephone": "978-369-9763","name": "Concord Museum","url": "","description": "Founded in 1886, the Concord Museum is a repository of local history and a gateway to the town of Concord. It educates visitors from around the world about the history of Concord and its continuing influence on American political, literary and cultural life through permanent and changing exhibits, educational programs and publications. Its collection contains a range of unique items from the area, including a Paul Revere lantern; many objects that once belonged to Henry David Thoreau; a reproduction of Ralph Waldo Emerson&apos;s study; and furniture, artwork, and objects from the colonial era on. "} Concord Museum 200 Lexington Road, Concord, MA 01742978-369-9763http://www.concordmuseum.org/[Details/Reserve]
Concord Museum Founded in 1886, the Concord Museum is a repository of local history and a gateway to the town of Concord. It educates visitors from around the world about the history of Concord and its continuing influence on American political, literary and cultural life through permanent and changing exhibits, educational programs and publications. Its collection contains a range of unique items from the area, including a Paul Revere lantern; many objects that once belonged to Henry David Thoreau; a reproduction of Ralph Waldo Emerson's study; and furniture, artwork, and objects from the colonial era on.
Categories: Family, History
Pass Benefits Each pass admits up to 4 visitors at $5-off each; valid year-round including during the Family Trees program. 
{"@context": "http://schema.org","@type": "CivicStructure","address": "177 Main Street, Acton, MA 01720","logo": "https://s3.amazonaws.com/ai-assets/museum-logos/ma-discovery-museums_4_md.jpg","telephone": "978-264-4200","name": "Discovery Museum","url": "","description": "Acton&apos;s Discovery Museum, located on a 4-acre campus, is aimed at providing an environment in which play becomes a conduit for learning about science, technology, and nature. The museum&apos;s building contains a number of galleries dedicated to teaching scientific concepts in a fun, hands-on environment, as well as themed play spaces for younger visitors. Outdoors, Discovery Woods includes a nature playscape with a cargo net climb, nest swing and beaver lodges, as well as a treehouse with an observation deck and a bouncy bridge. There are opportunities to practice orienteering with provided maps and instructions on trails on the property. A museum shop is on the premises. The museum building is fully ADA compliant, with exhibits based on Universal Design principles. "} Discovery Museum 177 Main Street, Acton, MA 01720978-264-4200http://www.discoveryacton.org/[Details/Reserve]
Discovery Museum Acton's Discovery Museum, located on a 4-acre campus, is aimed at providing an environment in which play becomes a conduit for learning about science, technology, and nature. The museum's building contains a number of galleries dedicated to teaching scientific concepts in a fun, hands-on environment, as well as themed play spaces for younger visitors. Outdoors, Discovery Woods includes a nature playscape with a cargo net climb, nest swing and beaver lodges, as well as a treehouse with an observation deck and a bouncy bridge. There are opportunities to practice orienteering with provided maps and instructions on trails on the property. A museum shop is on the premises. The museum building is fully ADA compliant, with exhibits based on Universal Design principles.
Categories: Children, Family, Science
Pass Benefits Patrons using library passes will pay 1/2 price admission per person (for each of the three people) which still allows access to the Discovery Museum, as well as Discovery Woods. Any people accompanying the 3 pass users will still pay the regular admission fee. 
{"@context": "http://schema.org","@type": "CivicStructure","address": "180 Hemenway Road, Framingham, MA 01701","logo": "https://s3.amazonaws.com/ai-assets/museum-logos/ma-native-plant-trust_12_md.jpg","telephone": "508-877-7630","name": "Garden in the Woods (Native Plant Trust)","url": "","description": "The Garden in the Woods (Native Plant Trust) displays rare and common native New England plants in a naturalistic setting at its location in Framingham, Massachusetts, attracting visitors from all over the world. Set on 45 acres, it includes two miles of paths that meander over glacier-sculpted ridges and through narrow valleys. A pond, a wooded bog, several springs, and a brook bring a diversity of wildlife into the garden. The garden is maintained by the Native Plant Trust, which works throughout New England to monitor and protect rare and endangered plants, collect and bank seeds for biological diversity, control invasive species, conduct research, and educate the public."} Garden in the Woods (Native Plant Trust) 180 Hemenway Road, Framingham, MA 01701508-877-7630http://www.nativeplanttrust.org/[Details/Reserve]
Garden in the Woods (Native Plant Trust) The Garden in the Woods (Native Plant Trust) displays rare and common native New England plants in a naturalistic setting at its location in Framingham, Massachusetts, attracting visitors from all over the world. Set on 45 acres, it includes two miles of paths that meander over glacier-sculpted ridges and through narrow valleys. A pond, a wooded bog, several springs, and a brook bring a diversity of wildlife into the garden. The garden is maintained by the Native Plant Trust, which works throughout New England to monitor and protect rare and endangered plants, collect and bank seeds for biological diversity, control invasive species, conduct research, and educate the public.
